insight: For New Zealand in the FIFA World Cup 2026, hydration breaks have had a mixed impact on their game momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, although they did manage to gain momentum in one out of the two breaks. The team's approach before the break also seems to influence the outcome, as when they were pressing their opponents, they lost momentum at a higher rate, whereas when they weren't pressing, they actually managed to gain some momentum after the break. This suggests that New Zealand may need to adjust their strategy before hydration breaks, particularly when they're on the front foot, to maintain their advantage and come out stronger after the pause.
